name: Kaspersky
tagline: Excellent protection engine with industry-leading threat intelligence
description: Kaspersky offers one of the most technically accomplished antivirus engines available, backed by one of the world's largest threat intelligence networks.
longDescription: Kaspersky's antivirus engine is among the most technically sophisticated available. The company operates one of the world's largest threat intelligence networks, analyzing over 360,000 new malware samples daily. This gives Kaspersky an edge in detecting emerging threats before they become widespread. In independent lab tests, Kaspersky consistently achieves 99%+ detection rates with reasonable system impact. However, the company's Russian origins have led to government bans in several countries. Kaspersky has since relocated much of its infrastructure to Switzerland and opened Transparency Centers for independent code reviews.

keyFeatures.name: Security Cloud Network
keyFeatures.description: Global threat intelligence network processing 360,000+ new samples daily. Enables real-time protection against emerging threats.

keyFeatures.name: Safe Money
keyFeatures.description: Browser hardening for online banking and shopping. Creates a secure, isolated environment with protected browser and virtual keyboard.

keyFeatures.name: Application Control
keyFeatures.description: Dynamic allowlisting that categorizes applications by trust level. Automatically restricts untrusted programs based on behavior.

keyFeatures.name: File Protection
keyFeatures.description: Multi-layered scanning engine combining signature, heuristic, and cloud-based detection. Can scan encrypted connections (HTTPS) for hidden threats.

verdict: Kaspersky offers technically excellent antivirus protection backed by unparalleled threat intelligence. The malware detection engine is among the best in the world. However, the geopolitical controversy surrounding the company's Russian origins is a legitimate concern that users should consider.

faqs.question: Is Kaspersky banned in the US?
faqs.answer: Yes, Kaspersky software is banned from US federal government systems. Private citizens can still purchase and use Kaspersky products, but the US government recommends against it.

faqs.question: Is Kaspersky VPN any good?
faqs.answer: Kaspersky's included VPN (Secure Connection) is quite limited with only 300MB of daily data on standard plans. It is adequate for occasional secure browsing but not suitable for streaming or heavy use.

author.name: Sarah Mitchell
author.title: Senior Tech Reviewer
author.bio: Sarah has been evaluating cybersecurity products for 7 years.

seoMeta.title: Kaspersky Review 2026: 8.4/10 Rating | TrueTopReviews
